title: Lacework Fund
compiled_truth:
Lacework Fund is a seed-stage venture capital firm focused exclusively on security and compliance infrastructure. Founded in late 2025 by [Ren Khalil](people/ren-khalil) and [Uri Ramirez](people/uri-ramirez), the fund carved out a niche early by betting on founders building at the intersection of data integrity, access control, and regulatory automation. Their thesis is pretty straightforward: as data infrastructure becomes more distributed, the attack surface for compliance failures grows exponentially, and most teams underinvest in these layers until it's too late.

The fund operates out of a small office in Austin, though Ren and Uri are both remote-first and spend most of their time between New York and Berlin. Their initial vehicle was modest — roughly $38M — but they've been disciplined about check sizes, typically leading or co-leading $1.5–3M seed rounds. Lacework Fund (sometimes just called "Lacework" internally, though the team dislikes the ambiguity with the name) has a portfolio of around fourteen companies as of mid-2028, spanning identity verification, audit trail tooling, encrypted pipeline orchestration, and deterministic data infrastructure.

Their investment in Anchor Codec came early — Q2 2026 — after [Uri Ramirez](people/uri-ramirez) saw a demo of the single-pass primitives at a private event in Brooklyn. Uri reportedly pushed hard for the deal internally, arguing that Anchor's approach to combining compression, authentication, and transmission collapsed several compliance-sensitive steps into one auditable operation. Ren was more cautious initially but came around after a technical deep-dive with Anchor's founding team.

Lacework's value-add goes beyond capital. They run a semi-annual "Compliance Infrastructure Summit" that's become a quiet but well-regarded gathering for seed-stage founders in the space. They also maintain a shared threat-modeling framework that portfolio companies can adapt — something that's been particularly useful for startups navigating emerging data sovereignity regulations in the EU. Their reputation is low-key but solid; they don't chase headlines, and their LPs tend to be institutional allocators with long time horizons. As of 2028, they're reportedly raising Fund II, targeting $65M.
timeline:
- **2025-11-15** | Lacework Fund officially closes Fund I at $38M, led by [Ren Khalil](people/ren-khalil) and [Uri Ramirez](people/uri-ramirez).
- **2026-02-08** | First investment announced — a $2.1M seed round in an audit trail tooling startup based in Toronto.
- **2026-05-19** | [Uri Ramirez](people/uri-ramirez) attends Brooklyn demo event and first encounters Anchor Codec's single-pass primitives.
- **2026-06-30** | Lacework Fund participates in Anchor Codec's seed round, co-leading alongside another institutional investor.
- **2026-10-12** | Inaugural Compliance Infrastructure Summit held in Austin; ~40 attendees from portfolio companies and prospects.
- **2027-03-22** | Portfolio hits ten companies. Internal memo circulated on thesis refinement around deterministic data infrastructure.
- **2027-07-14** | [Ren Khalil](people/ren-khalil) publishes essay on compliance-by-default architectures, widely shared in infra founder circles.
- **2027-11-03** | Second annual Compliance Infrastructure Summit, this time in Berlin. Anchor Codec presents on authentication-in-transit patterns.
- **2028-02-18** | Lacework Fund begins fundraising for Fund II, targeting $65M.
- **2028-06-01** | Portfolio reaches fourteen companies; two have progressed to Series A rounds.

_facts.name: Lacework Fund
_facts.description: Seed fund with focus on security and compliance infrastructure
